name: pAce
description: Reverse-polarity variant of Ace-mNeon2 whose fluorescence increases during depolarization, enabling dual-polarity multiplexed voltage imaging in a single color channel.

spectrum.name: pAce
spectrum.note: pAce's fluorescent reporter is mNeonGreen (the Ace2 opsin is the dark FRET acceptor). The stored excitation/emission arrays are the FPbase mNeonGreen curve verbatim (identical to the Ace2N-mNeon2 page; peaks ex 506 / em 517). No separately measured pAce curve is published.

kinetics.note: Amplitude-weighted mean of the bi-exponential fit at 22°C in spiking HEK cells: τ_on = 0.82×0.51 + 0.18×1.5 = 0.69 ms; τ_off = 0.78×0.61 + 0.22×3.2 = 1.18 ms (fast/slow components and amplitudes from Table S1).

dynamicRangeData.note: ΔF/F per 100 mV step in spiking HEK cells at 22°C (Table S1); main text reports 36.6 ± 0.7% ΔF/F per 120 mV step (n=11 cells)

ΔF/F₀ = 0.41 ± 0.14 for a 100-mV step (−70 to +30 mV, 100 ms) in ND7/23 cells under 1P wide-field illumination (470 nm LED, 10 mW/mm², 50 Hz), whole-cell voltage clamp. Measured alongside Jarvis (0.29) and JEDI-2P (−0.46).

sensitivityData.note: ΔF/F per single action potential (mean ± SEM 10.7 ± 1.4%, n=87 spikes) in VIP+ interneurons in mouse primary visual cortex (V1), in vivo, 1P widefield epifluorescence (400/600 Hz). Companion per-spike values in the same experiment: Ace-mNeon2 −16.5%, VARNAM2 −7.3%, pAceR 3.2%.

ΔF/F₀ per AP = 0.43 ± 0.04 at 1 kHz acquisition in organotypic hippocampal slices under 1P wide-field illumination (470 nm LED, 16 mW/mm²). Rises with frame rate: 0.33 (0.5 kHz), 0.43 (1 kHz), 0.51 (2 kHz). Measured alongside Jarvis.

pAce: 19.9 ± 1.8% ΔF/F0 per AP (n=5 cells) in cultured neurons at 484 Hz under 1P epifluorescence (488 nm, 4 W/cm²), measured in the HVI+ paper as the spectrally similar green positive-going reference (Table S2; per-100 mV ΔF/F0 for pAce was not determined). Value also appears in Fig. 2E.

apWidthData.sample: Mouse, V1 VIP+ interneurons, awake, in vivo
apWidthData.note: Optical single-AP FWHM digitized from the mean ± SEM spike waveform in Fig. 1I (right sub-panel, the light-green positive-going pAce trace; fast rise + slower decay), recorded in awake mouse V1 VIP+ interneurons in vivo under 1P epifluorescence at 400 Hz with 4×4 binning. FWHM measured at half of the peak ΔF/F deflection, calibrated to the panel's 5 ms scale bar (35 px = 5 ms). At 400 Hz the frame interval is 2.5 ms, so this width is frame-rate-broadened — treat it as an upper bound on the intrinsic optical response (underlying step kinetics τ ≈ 0.7/1.2 ms). Companion waveforms in the same panel: Ace-mNeon2, VARNAM2, pAceR.

apWidthData.sample: Mouse, primary hippocampal neurons, in vitro
apWidthData.note: Cross-GEVI entry from the mBaoJin/Electra benchmarking paper (Zhang et al. 2025). Optical single-AP FWHM digitized from the averaged spike waveform generated in response to 1-AP electrical stimulation in primary mouse hippocampal neuronal culture (Fig. 1j, top-left cyan pAce panel), recorded at ~1 kHz field-stim imaging (1006 Hz per Methods) under 1P wide-field illumination at room temperature (22°C). FWHM measured at half of the peak |ΔF/F₀| deflection, calibrated to the panel's shared 50 ms scale bar (64 px = 50 ms → ~1.28 px/ms). Companion averaged waveforms in the same panel: ElectraON (orange), Ace-mNeon2 (green), ElectraOFF (gold). This width is frame-rate-broadened at 1 kHz (1 ms frame interval).

brightnessData.note: Resting fluorescence normalized to Ace-mNeon in HEK cells (Fig. S11A)
Per-neuron relative brightness in primary rat cortical neurons (Kv2.1-tagged constructs), normalized to ASAP4e-Kv = 1.0

photostabilityData.note: Digitized F/F0 at 10 min ≈ 0.38 from the photobleaching curve in HEK cells under continuous 505 nm illumination (Fig. S11B, teal pAce curve); pAce supported extended 30-min continuous imaging at ~5 mW/mm² with stable d' (Fig. S14). See the photobleach[] entry for the full digitized curve.

pAce lost 80% of initial fluorescence (retained ~20%) under continuous 10 mW/mm² wide-field illumination in HeLa cells (n=14 cells); measured as a reference alongside ElectraON/ElectraOFF in the mBaoJin/Electra GEVI paper. Duration read from the Fig. 1h x-axis: the pAce (cyan) curve reaches ~20% F/F0 near the ~2400 s (~40 min) endpoint (digitized directly; see the photobleach[] Fig. 1h entry).

F/F0 remaining after 1 min at 40 mW/mm² (1P widefield, 470 nm, CHO cells), read from the pAce 40 mW/mm² bleach curve in Fig. 1F; falls to ~0.47 by 235 s. Measured alongside Jarvis (~92% at 1 min, far more photostable) and JEDI-2P (~71%) at the same 40 mW/mm².

photobleach.note: Model-free photostability: t75% = time for fluorescence to fall to 75% of initial. Digitized from the pAce 40 mW/mm² curve in Fig. 1F (1P widefield, 470 nm, CHO cells), F/F0 over ~235 s; t75 read from the digitized points. Bi-exponential fit. 1P/40 mW/mm² (intensityMWmm2=40); the panel scales t75 to the 100 mW/mm² reference.

Cross-GEVI entry from the Electra benchmarking paper (a different recording from the Fig. 1F entry above): photobleaching of the fluorescence baseline over a 30-min continuous 1P recording at 5 mW/mm² in cultured primary hippocampal neurons (Fig. S4h, the cyan pAce curve — sensor colors confirmed against the labeled bars in Fig. S4f/g: pAce cyan, ElectraON orange, Ace-mNeon2 green, ElectraOFF gold). Stretched exponential F = exp(−(t/2287)^0.974), t₇₅% ≈ 636 s (10.6 min). Companion curves (same figure): ElectraON, ElectraOFF, Ace-mNeon2.

Origin-paper photobleaching (Fig. S11B, left/green panel, the teal pAce curve) in HEK cells under continuous 505 nm LED illumination at 25 mW/mm² (n=4 wells, ~100 cells/well). Digitized by HSV color separation, verified against the figure; biexponential fit, model-free t75% ≈ 68 s. Companion curves in the same panel: Ace-mNeon, Ace-mNeon2. (A third independent recording, distinct from the Fig. 1F and Fig. S4h entries above.)

Cross-GEVI entry from the mBaoJin/Electra benchmarking paper (Zhang et al. 2025): photobleaching of the fluorescence baseline in HeLa cells under continuous 10 mW/mm² wide-field illumination over ~2400 s (Fig. 1h, cyan pAce curve; n=14 cells). Digitized directly by cyan/teal color separation with black-tick axis calibration (x: 0-2500 s, y: 0-100%), verified against the figure; the pAce curve falls to ~20% F/F0 by ~2400 s, consistent with the paper's 'pAce lost 80% of initial fluorescence'. Biexponential fit, model-free t75% ≈ 325 s (t50% ≈ 834 s). A fourth independent recording, distinct from the Fig. 1F (40 mW/mm²), Fig. S4h (5 mW/mm²), and Fig. S11B (25 mW/mm²) entries; same source+figure as the ElectraON/ElectraOFF Fig. 1h family so panels cross-overlay. Companion curves in the same panel: ElectraON, Ace-mNeon2, ElectraOFF.

twoPhoton.note: Demonstrated 2P-compatible: scanless holographic 2P voltage imaging detected APs in organotypic slices and high-contrast AP detection in awake mouse cortex; FRET-opsin sensors showed improved performance with low-irradiance scanless illumination.
